title: prunus cerasifera var. iranica
intro: Prunus cerasifera var. iranica, also known as the Iranian cherry plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Middle East, with showy white flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Prunus cerasifera var. iranica include potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular-protective effects, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, or extracts of its leaves, bark, or fruit, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
